_comment: Realistic 5-day persona script. Mixes durable facts, noise, contradictions, and references to prior days. Used by the brutal test harness — no conversation lines are hardcoded in Python.
name: Caroline
background: Therapist switching jobs in Seattle, has a recurring weekly support group, climbs rocks, just got a new pet.

days.label: monday_morning
turns: Caroline: Hi, my name is Caroline. I work at GreenLeaf Cafe.
Caroline: I live in Seattle, in the Capitol Hill neighborhood.
Caroline: My favorite hobby is rock climbing.
Caroline: I have a cat named Mochi.
Caroline: lol
Caroline: Going to grab coffee, brb
Caroline: I went to the LGBTQ support group on May 7th, 2023.
Caroline: The support group meets on Sundays.

days.label: monday_evening
turns: Caroline: Did you watch the Mariners game last night?
Caroline: Anyway, my friend Melanie also goes to the support group.
Caroline: Melanie and I are climbing buddies too.
Caroline: hmm, I should make dinner soon

days.label: tuesday_morning
turns: Caroline: Quick update — I left GreenLeaf Cafe last month. I'm at TechCorp now.
Caroline: TechCorp is way more demanding but the pay is much better.
Caroline: Met Melanie for coffee this morning.
Caroline: Coffee, the only thing keeping me going lol

days.label: wednesday
turns: Caroline: I went to the support group on Sunday, like usual.
Caroline: We talked about boundaries at work, very relevant for me at TechCorp.
Caroline: I should mention — I also use Datadog at TechCorp for monitoring.
Caroline: Datadog dashboards are my new best friend.
Caroline: Melanie mentioned she's getting a dog.
Caroline: Random thought, I love rainy days.

days.label: thursday
turns: Caroline: Long day. Datadog alerts woke me up at 3am.
Caroline: yeah
Caroline: I'm still going to the support group every Sunday — that part hasn't changed.
Caroline: My cat Mochi has been extra needy this week.

days.label: friday
turns: Caroline: Hobby update: I stopped rock climbing for now. I picked up sculpting instead.
Caroline: TechCorp asked me to lead a Snowflake migration.
Caroline: Snowflake is everywhere at this company.
Caroline: Snowflake queries are expensive though, watch out for that.
Caroline: I picked up Snowflake basics from the onboarding docs.

expected_recall.description: Day-1 self-introduction (name) must survive to day 5
Day-1 location must survive to day 5
Day-2 employer change should win over day-1 employer

expected_curiosity_gaps.description: Datadog mentioned multiple times, agent should ingest a brief if curiosity is enabled
